Title: A fungal endophyte helps plants to tolerate root herbivory through changes in gibberellin and jasmonate signaling

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein; jasmonate receptor complex component
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates herbivore-induced signaling and growth inhibition responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced COI1 function in coi1-18 abolishes rice water weevil-induced growth reduction and enhances growth promotion by Piriformospora indica; JA signaling also limits endophyte-mediated increase in larval survival
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis using JA-insensitive coi1-18 plants in split-root experiments; biomass phenotyping; larval survival assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Piriformospora indica root inoculation; rice water weevil adult leaf feeding; larval root herbivory; split-root system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1-18 plants did not show the RWW-induced biomass reduction observed in WT, indicating JA signaling mediates herbivore-induced growth inhibition. 2. P. indica promoted growth more strongly in coi1-18 than in WT, indicating JA signaling negatively regulates endophyte growth promotion. 3. Endophyte increased larval survival in coi1-18, suggesting JA signaling restricts endophyte-associated susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating COI1/JA perception could reduce growth penalties from root herbivory and enhance growth promotion by beneficial endophytes, but may risk increased susceptibility under some endophyte contexts
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: JA-insensitive plants may alter defense outputs; in this study endophyte increased larval survival in coi1-18
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although COI1 is a signaling component rather than TF/enzyme, it is a core experimental gene because mutant analysis was central to demonstrating JA pathway involvement.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 involved in GA catabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth and antagonizes jasmonate-mediated growth inhibition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Eui1 overexpression lowers GA and blocks Piriformospora indica-mediated growth promotion; Eui1-OX plants show stronger growth inhibition under root herbivory, indicating GA counteracts herbivore-induced growth suppression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant/overexpression analysis using GA-deficient Eui1-OX plants in split-root experiments; biomass phenotyping
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Piriformospora indica root inoculation; rice water weevil adult leaf feeding; larval root herbivory
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Eui1-OX plants were not growth-promoted by P. indica, indicating GA signaling is required for endophyte mutualism. 2. Eui1-OX plants showed reduced shoot and infested-root biomass after larval attack, supporting a protective role of GA against herbivore-induced growth inhibition. 3. The study uses Eui1-OX to functionally place GA opposite JA in the interaction.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing GA catabolism or avoiding EUI1 overactivity may help preserve growth and tolerance benefits from beneficial endophytes under herbivory
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower GA in Eui1-OX causes dwarfism and loss of endophyte-mediated growth benefits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior literature for EUI1 function in GA catabolism; here Eui1-OX is used as a functional genetic tool to establish GA dependence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes ent-copalyl diphosphate to ent-kaurene in the GA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control point not definitively established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth and tolerance to herbivory by antagonizing JA-mediated growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P. indica induces OsKS1 transcription in roots, whereas root herbivory suppresses it; this supports endophyte activation of GA biosynthesis during tolerance to root herbivory
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of roots under endophyte and herbivore treatments; integration with mutant phenotypes from Eui1-OX
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots at harvest; induced by P. indica and reduced by RWW larvae
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Piriformospora indica colonization; rice water weevil larval root herbivory; adult leaf herbivory
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P. indica alone elicited ~20% higher OsKS1 transcript abundance in roots. 2. RWW larvae reduced OsKS1 transcript abundance by ~54% in roots. 3. OsKS1 expression patterns support GA pathway activation by the endophyte and suppression by herbivory.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upregulating OsKS1 or maintaining its induction under herbivory may enhance GA-supported compensatory root and shoot growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess GA pathway activation may alter plant architecture or defense balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study measured transcript abundance only; GA metabolites themselves were not directly quantified.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: JA conjugation to active hormone JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates jasmonic acid to isoleucine to form bioactive JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key activation step for JA signaling; rate-limiting not established here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates jasmonate signaling associated with herbivore responses and growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsJAR1 transcript abundance in roots was not significantly altered by endophyte or herbivory, despite JA and JA-Ile changes, suggesting de novo JA-Ile biosynthesis via OsJAR1 was not a major regulated step at harvest
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in roots; hormone profiling of JA and JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots at harvest; no significant treatment effect, with only a trend for endophyte × larvae interaction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Piriformospora indica colonization; rice water weevil larval root herbivory; adult leaf herbivory
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsJAR1 transcript abundance was not significantly affected by endophyte, adults, or larvae. 2. JA-Ile increased in roots after larval feeding despite unchanged OsJAR1 expression. 3. The data suggest that de novo JA pathway transcription was not strongly active at harvest.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less directly supported for engineering in this paper; monitoring OsJAR1 may help distinguish hormone accumulation from local biosynthetic transcription
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating JA-Ile formation could alter defense-growth balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was a primary assayed pathway gene in the Results, even though treatment effects were limited.
